metadata.note: Military rule, its consolidation instruments, and the sovereigntist realignment. Gated on AFRICA-SAHEL alone -- a purely internal phenomenon; any external centroid on the OR-gate would admit foreign-only titles. DROPPED after pre-audit: putsch (0 EN hits; kept in de/ru where it is the idiom), Deby (0), Tchiani (1, and that title is a Türkiye story -> patron contest), transition (1, a Senegal LNG story, and generic), sovereignty EN (0), press freedom (1, Niger STATE in Nigeria), election (2, Guinea-Bissau). ECOWAS/CEDEAO/Alliance of Sahel States ALL measured 0-2 hits: the AES-vs-ECOWAS bloc rupture that Phase 1 sized at ~22 does not survive the exact matcher -- that number came from a loose regex that was counting the AES Corp utility. Recorded honestly rather than keyword-stuffed. DROPPED on re-measure: the dissolve/dissoudre/dissolution verb family -- it added only ~4 titles over the 'political parties' noun and two of them were off-theater ('Senegal's president dismisses prime minister, dissolves government', 'Guinea dissolves 40 political parties'). Keep the object noun, drop the generic verb (the Colombia lesson: neutral RESULT words leak). DROPPED on sample-read: bare 'coup' (EN). The word-START matcher is case-insensitive and language-blind, so an EN alias fires on FRENCH titles -- 'coup' caught 'CAN 2025 : coup de tonnerre, le Sénégal destitué' (a football story). French is a third of this theater's corpus, so EN-list short words must be checked against French idiom (coup de tonnerre / coup d'envoi / coup dur), not just against English. 'coup' added almost no unique recall -- its real hits also matched 'junte', 'Goita' or 'Africa Corps'. Kept the unambiguous fr 'coup d'État' instead.
